participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in model compression, distillation, and deployment on edge devices; experienced in project scoping and industry consultancy.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to establish a spin-off that commercializes low-cost AI for SMEs, and to mentor the next generation of AI engineers in Singapore.

participants.name: Siti (ku1rz7)
participants.entity_name: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-ku1rz76a
participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-ku1rz76a
participants.professional_persona: Siti is a community health nurse at a polyclinic in Woodlands. She sees AI as a way to streamline patient triage and free up nurses for more empathetic care.

participants.professional_persona: Computer vision engineer at a smart-city solutions provider, developing traffic monitoring and crowd-density models for public agencies. Passionate about using AI for urban sustainability.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in object detection, tracking algorithms, and video analytics at the edge.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aims to contribute to Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and later advise other cities on AI deployment for public good.

participants.name: Dr. (0j8nff)

participants.skills_and_expertise: Deep expertise in transformer architectures, causal inference for NLP, and responsible AI frameworks.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to publish foundational work in reasoning AI and co-develop industry guidelines for trustworthy AI in finance and healthcare.

participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Wants to scale the startup’s AI offerings regionally and eventually launch a fund that invests in responsible AI ventures.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group discussed how activities like enterprise outreach, matchmaking, training, and platform onboarding drive the 100E programme's goals, with a focus on balancing quality and quantity of engagements. There was consensus on moderate estimates for outreach sessions (30-40, central tendency ~35), matchmaking events (8-12, central ~10), training workshops (15-25, central ~20), and onboarding assists (40-60, central ~50), reflecting a shared view that 50 trainers and S$500M funding support a balanced rollout. Disagreements centered on the pace of adoption across sectors: some argued for higher numbers to maximize engagement, while others emphasized lower, more targeted efforts for slower-adopting sectors like healthcare. The lead practitioner's higher estimates (40, 12, 25, 60) contrasted with Siti's lower ones (30, 8, 15, 40), with most participants converging on intermediate values (35, 10, 20, 50) to ensure quality and feasibility.
